characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Abusive Parents: It seems to run in both sides of their family. Clay often takes his son into his study and belts him whenever his exploits causeHilarity to Ensuebefore giving out aSpoof Aesopbased off bigoted 1950s beliefs.He gets even worse as time goes on.In one of those, he instinctively reaches for his belt even though Orel hadn't done anything wrong and had to (innocently) have this pointed out.In one episode, Orel makes a stop motion film that shows his everyday life and what he has learned from the town’s teachings. One of the things that stand out the most is the portrayal of his father Clay,who is shown as a drunk, snarling wolf, which is coupled with some discouraging words and a scene in whicha belt (in a way a serpent would) chases and warps around a figurine which is meant to represent Orelignites a comment from Dr. Potterswheel questioning if his father was actually molesting him. The abuse gets very horrifying without being sexual, as shown in the episode "Nature". It's implied this symbolism of Clay being a wolf is to show Orel is actually afraid of Clay, who is monstrous.The Wolf:I need to teach you what you did wrong by beating you in my study, because that’s what I do in the study to you; I beat you, and then I lecture you, and that’s it nothing else. Come here, Orel, as you pull your pants up from my spanking.Blobertaleaves her kids unsupervisedand provides them with dangerous tools to keep them entertained. When Orel comes from school covered in bruises and blood,she’s far more concerned with the fact that the supplies budget is gonna rise than with Orel’s state, and she doesn't realize it when her younger son Shapey gets switched with another kid for a long time.After his mother Angela's death,Clay's father Arthur briefly becomes this. Not that it excuses it, but Clay was a spoiled brat who was responsible for his wife's death, and even then, Clay's birth resulted in Angela becoming religiously fanatic about him, her "miracle baby". Following

characters.name: As a whole
characters.description: Abusive Parents: It seems to run in both sides of their family. Clay often takes his son into his study and belts him whenever his exploits causeHilarity to Ensuebefore giving out aSpoof Aesopbased off bigoted 1950s beliefs.He gets even worse as time goes on.In one of those, he instinctively reaches for his belt even though Orel hadn't done anything wrong and had to (innocently) have this pointed out.In one episode, Orel makes a stop motion film that shows his everyday life and what he has learned from the town’s teachings. One of the things that stand out the most is the portrayal of his father Clay,who is shown as a drunk, snarling wolf, which is coupled with some discouraging words and a scene in whicha belt (in a way a serpent would) chases and warps around a figurine which is meant to represent Orelignites a comment from Dr. Potterswheel questioning if his father was actually molesting him. The abuse gets very horrifying without being sexual, as shown in the episode "Nature". It's implied this symbolism of Clay being a wolf is to show Orel is actually afraid of Clay, who is monstrous.The Wolf:I need to teach you what you did wrong by beating you in my study, because that’s what I do in the study to you; I beat you, and then I lecture you, and that’s it nothing else. Come here, Orel, as you pull your pants up from my spanking.Blobertaleaves her kids unsupervisedand provides them with dangerous tools to keep them entertained. When Orel comes from school covered in bruises and blood,she’s far more concerned with the fact that the supplies budget is gonna rise than with Orel’s state, and she doesn't realize it when her younger son Shapey gets switched with another kid for a long time.After his mother Angela's death,Clay's father Arthur briefly becomes this. Not that it excuses it, but Clay was a spoiled brat who was responsible for his wife's death, and even then, Clay's birth resulted in Angela becoming religiously fanatic about him, her "miracle baby". Following

characters.name: Orel Puppington
characters.description: The show's protagonist. Sweet as a button and unapologetically innocent, especially compared to most of the residents of his hometown. This innocence often leads him to do rather... warped things,usually due to how he interprets his father's lessons and Reverend Putty's sermons. In spite of this, his good nature is never in doubt, and he's consistently portrayed as always wanting the best for others. Absurdly Youthful Mother: Gender-inverted. In "God's Chef", in order to masturbate and not sin at the same time, he fills his pastry bag with his semen, breaks into women's homes,and fills them up with his baby batter. This causes a string of unexpected pregnancies, and aFreeze-Frame Bonusin "Alone" implies it's still canon, even whenCerebus Syndromehas taken over. Orel's age is also barely in the double-digits. Adorably Precocious Child: Seriously, the kid wants to help anybody whenever possible. Too bad most people in Moralton are assholes, atbest. The All-American Boy:A MAJORDeconstructionof one. At first, he fits the bill of a typical child from a loving, Christian family, but once he becomes wise to how horrible many of the people around him, ESPECIALLYhis dad, are, his chipper attitudemorphs into a coping mechanism. He's stilla good kid, in spite of it all, though it's clear that the revelations regarding the people he once admired and respected have rendered him a lot more bitter. Ambiguous Innocence: He is an interesting example. Orel is generally a good kid who always attempts and likes to do well. He wants to help everyone around him, is open to different perspectives, and doesn’t judge people for their appearances or beliefs. He's described as being pure and innocent by his grandpa and the Reverend, but this innocence makes him unable to distinguish what is morally right from what is morally wrong. This, coupled with the adults' dubious advice, lack of good role models, and his town’s backward morality, results in Orel causing a lot of harm, sometimes doing o

characters.name: Bloberta Puppington
characters.description: Clay's wife and Orel and Shapey's mom, who married Clay to keep from being theOld Maidand to get away from her own dysfunctional family... only to end up in an evenmoredysfunctional family in the process. She'sdistressingly neglectfultowards her children at the best of times, and at worst, she's seemingly desparate to find anybody who can make her feel less empty and miserable on a daily basis. A Lighter Shade of Black: Bloberta is obviouslyfarfrom a saint, though she's merely a neglectfulHypocritewho goes out of her way to cheat on her husband whenever possible. She's still a better person than Clay himself, who, among other things, isutterly fucking NUTS, physically, as well as emotionally abusive, and aterriblemayor. Adults Are Useless: APushover Parentto the core, and someone who never really has a notable impact in the town in general, Bloberta is a textbook example. Alcoholic Parent: Isn't this as a parent, but used to be this herself before meeting Clay. She now throws herself into compulsively cleaning while her husband became the alcoholic. Always Someone Better: Bloberta's siblings, according to her mother, and all her friends who were getting married, which prompted her to trap Clay into a relationship.

characters.name: Bartholomew
characters.description: Orel's beloved pet dog. His first (and last) appearance is in the Season 2 episode "Love." The Ace: Bartholomew learns tricks quickly and is even smart enough to retrieve Orel's favorite lucky Bible when Shapey takes it. All-Loving Hero: Bartholomew gets along with everyone. Even cats! Canine Companion: During the brief time Orel has Bartholomew, they're rarely seen apart. Canine Loyalty: A good, loyal dog to Orel.

characters.name: Arthur Puppington
characters.description: Clay's formerly abusive dad and Angela's husband. Compared with his estranged son, he's a doting grandpa to Orel. Cruel Mercy: Arthur subjected Clay to this, declaring he wasn't worth disciplining after causing his mother's death. To say this messed Clay up is an understatement. Disappointed in You: He likely felt this towards Clay for causing Angela's death despite being told his mother had a weak heart and yet pulled that cruel prank on her (although to be fair, he didn't expect his mom to die from it). Doting Grandparent: Unlikehis past treatment of Clay, Arthur is a loving and caring grandfather to Orel.Becauseherealizedjust how much he messed up Clay and wanted to have a better relationship with his grandson. Plus, Orel being a good if naive, kid as well, seeing he was different than Clay with no baggage he caused to Arthur helped a lot with their bonding. Flashback with the Other Darrin: In "Beforel Orel," a clip from the Season 3 episode "Passing" is shown in which Arthur declares that Clay is not worth disciplining, redubbed with Joe Unger as an older Arthur.

characters.name: Angela Puppington
characters.description: Clay's mom, who had ten stillbirths and passed on the "Lost Commandments" to Clay. Comically Missing the Point: Adarkly comedicexample: She attributes Clay being born alive and well to her prayers rather than the fact that she didn't smoke or do anything else incredibly stupid while pregnant Deadly Prank: Clay was so upset at learning that he wasn't her "precious only ever" that he faked committing suicide, which ended up causing her to have a fatal heart attack. Doting Parent: She spoiled Clay rotten to the point of ignoring her husband due to how all of her previous children were stillborn. Entertainingly Wrong: She attributes Clay's successful birth after ten stillbirths to all her prayers. She did pray so much that praying took up most of her spare time, and didn't do those wildly stupid things while pregnant with Clay.

characters.name: Doughy Latchkey
characters.description: Orel's best friend and the only son of Karl and Kim, who frequently ignore him. He's consistently shown to bea little down on his luck, though he remains a loyal, kind-hearted friend and good-natured boy in spite of it all. Abusive Parents: His parents are mainly neglectful, but the few times they interact, it's always to berate him about how he's ruining their fun and how they'd like it better if he weren't around. All for Nothing: In“Trigger”,Doughy picks up theJerkass Balland goes out of his way to belittle and show up Orel in order to go on Clay Puppington’s hunting trip, such as taking Orel’s spot at the Puppington dinner table, insulting his shooting skills, andsmoking cigarettes with Clay.However,it turned out that all Clay was doing was manipulating a vulnerable Doughy, by encouraging him to replace Orel, to get Orel better at shooting.Meaning that Clay never cared for him. Butt-Monkey: He's subject to a lot of physical pratfalls, especially at Hurtball. A Day in the Limelight: While Doughy normally serves as Orel’sHeterosexual Life PartnerandNumber Two, he’s the main protagonist of“Courtship”,“Trigger”, and“Abstinence”.

characters.name: Tommy Littler
characters.description: One of Orel's friends. Big Damn Heroes: When he sees his friends from the “retarded” group getting pelted with rocks, he immediately rushes over to protect them with his helmet. Good Is Not Soft: Tommy is definitely more morally upright and pleasant than most characters in the show, and will defend his friends when he can. That doesn’t mean you should test your luck with him; he will kick your ass should you wrong anyone he cares about or himself (his friends are not an exception to the latter). Jerkass Ball: He's generally a decent kid, but he holds this inBeforel Orel, where he took advantage of Orel's naivety by making him perform dangerous stunts for amusement. Nice Guy: He’s a decent kid who’s shown to have a good moral compass and never really goes beyond playfully teasing Orel, but his temper is clearly a bit more volatile than the rest of Orel’s friend group. For example, in God Fearinghe’s quite brutal in beating Doughy(granted, he’s under the assumption he took his toy but still).

characters.name: "Kid One"
characters.description: A friend of Orel’s who appeared often throughout Season 1; he has no given name, but is referred to in scripts for theMoral Orelas "Kid One". Ambiguous Situation: InElemental Orel, the kid can be seen with a black eye, but it’s not shown what caused it, nor is it discussed. Bully Turned Buddy: He was a bully to Orel when they were kids inBeforel Orel,thankfully he stopped doing this over time. Though he is still a bully, as can be seen inGod’s Blunders. Depending on the Writer: His personality isn’t always consistent between episodes. In most episodes, he’s about as nice as Doughy or Billy, inWaste, he’s shown to be rather annoyed at Orel’s comments while he and the rest of their troop go camping. InGod’s Blunders, he even teams up with Joe to bully theretardedkids, despite Tommy being a part of that group. The Dragon: Serves as one to Joe inGod’s Blunders, being the second most prominent bully in Joe’s group.

characters.name: Joe Secondopinionson
characters.description: Coach Stopframe's nephew. He frequently ping-pongs between acting as another one of Orel's friends, and a merciless, borderline sociopathicbullytowards him and everyone else.It's not until the final season where it becomes clear just how troubled he really is, deep down. Actually Pretty Funny: While Joe initially hijacks the microphones during Orel's movie to mock it, he genuinely enjoys the scenes recounting Orel's brief crack addiction from "Charity." At least until Orel complains that Joe is ruining the movie, then Joe goes back to hating it. Annoying Younger Sibling: His constant behavior with his older sister, which also includes making fun of her masculine voice and calling her ugly. Although compared to some older siblings, she doesn't really fight back against it. Attention Whore: His bullying antics are just to make sure all eyes are on him. Bastard Bastard: He’s a bullying shitheel born fromNurse Bendybeingraped by his father.

characters.name: Billy Figurelli
characters.description: Another one of Orel's friends, and Sal Figurelli's son. Borrowed Catchphrase: Billy sometimes uses his father’s catchphrase, “No prob!” Chain of Harm:Played for Laughsas Susie, Billy’s sister, slaps him across the face after seeing their mom do the same to their father. Children Are Innocent: Like Orel, he’s quite innocent; he came to believe that segregation was a good thing and didn’t even bat an eye at anything until the tail end. Demoted to Extra: He was originally in Orel's circle of friends, but he isn't seen as much after "Elemental Orel", only making cameo appearances from then on. The only involvement with Orel seems to be in "Innocence", but even then, his screen time was cut very short.

characters.name: Marionetta Jones
characters.description: Hair of Gold, Heart of Gold: Marionetta is a natural blonde who gives food to the sick and needy and is normally quite friendly. Mistaken for Thief: Orel continually believes throughoutElemental Orelthat Marionetta must have stolen the church’s donation money, despite the fact that she wasn’t even at church at the time. Nice Girl: Like most of Orel’s friends, she’s pretty kindhearted. She gives out cripple food to the needy and is rather patient with Orel’s idiocy regarding the stolen money. Despite being accused of stealing by Orel, she nonetheless tries to show him that it was Joe, proving she also cares about justice.

characters.name: Christina Posabule
characters.description: Orel'sDistaff Counterpartand mutual crush. Abusive Parents: Though only shown as a background event, her father also beats her with his belt the same way Clay beats Orel. Cheerful Child: Just as cheerful as Orel since they're both similar in personality. Distaff Counterpart: To Orel, as they both come from hardcore Protestant families, have an optimistic attitude, and are voiced by the same actress. Rev. Putty even refers to her as Orel's "little Orelette." Happily Married:To Orel in the far off finale. Unlike Clay and Bloberta, their marriage is completely fulfilling, and they are also loving parents to their children.

characters.name: Block Posabule
characters.description: Christina's younger brother, even more of a carbon copy of Shapey than she is of Orel. Bratty Half-Pint: Just like Shapey, he is spoiled and screams just as often. Fiery Redhead: He is a redhead who is just as loud and bratty as his adopted brother, Shapey. Good Counterpart: It’s implied he’s one of Shapey, given he’s slightly less bratty and would eventually cause Shapey to mellow out. Happily Adopted: In spite of both of his sets of parents being horrendously neglective, he couldn't be happier having a new brother in Shapey.

characters.name: Coach Stopframe
characters.description: Orel's gym teacher. Isincredibly depravedand promiscuous, despite his mellow demeanor and soft spot for Orel. Affably Evil: He's a pretty chill guy for someone who has orgies and takes part in Satanic rituals in his spare time. Ambiguously Related: To Joe Secondopinionson, who is apparently his nephew. Since he doesn't appear to have any particular connection to the Secondopinionsons and had her number, a popular fan theory is that he isNurse Bendy's (Joe's mother) brother. Arc Symbol: Rotating crosses, to symbolize his wishy-washy religious affiliation. Broken Tears:When he sees Clay cheating on him with Censordoll he realizes that deep down Clay never cared about him and he only goes by what brings him pleasure.

characters.name: Reverend Putty
characters.description: The Moralton church's snarky, hypocritical pastor. Lonely, seen as a joke by his peers, and desparate to find a woman who will relieve him of his misery. Big Good:Well... byMoral Orelstandards, at least.He's really not much better than the other adults, though he at least has something resembling a basic conscience, and is ultimately shown to care for Orel, despite his frequent irritation at the latter's wide-eyed devotion to Christ.During Season 3, he becomes a straightforward example, thanks to a little help from Orel, and being able to bond with his daughter. Character Development: One of the few adults to unambiguously change for the better. This is greatly shown in "Innocence", where he realizes that while Orel is at fault for all the chaos he has caused throughout town, he knows not to pin itallon Orel, as the adults he gets his misinformed advice from are also responsible. Chick Magnet: Has had at least 4 womennoteBloberta,Dottie,Florence, and in a more ambiguous case,Gladys Foamwirelust after him on-screen, which is pretty surprising for a man who's typically portrayed as a lonely grump. Even in a scrapped episode, Nurse Bendy, for once, has passionate sex with him (ironically, it was one-sided as he wasn't into it) after he tells her he doesn't think she's dumb but smart-defying, which actually turns her on. Say what you want about the guy, but when he wants to be, he can be pretty damn charming. Deadpan Snarker: His sermons are peppered with all kinds of jabs at the holy book, his miserable life, and the people he deals with on a daily basis.

characters.name: Stephanie Foamwire-Putty
characters.description: The atheistic owner of Buried Pleasures and the most rational person in the entire town. Butch Lesbian: Lots of piercings, has short hair, always wearing black, and runs a sex shop. Need we say more? Character Tics: She makes new piercings on herself whenever she gets too emotional. Cool Big Sis: To Orel as she gives him much saner advice than anyone else in all of Moralton. Dark Is Not Evil: Hell, she's a downright pleasant individual and thenicestperson out of the rest of the town.

characters.name: Miss Sculptham
characters.description: The schoolteacher who Doughy had a crush on and really doesn't seem to like her job. Apathetic Teacher: You can barely call what she does teaching in the first place, and she always carries a bored tone when speaking with her students. Beehive Hairdo: Her hairstyle. Child by Rape:She becomes pregnant after being raped by Creepler. She later aborts it. Deadpan Snarker: She's really bored and uncaring while on the clock, so her students get a lot of snarky lessons.

characters.name: Nurse Bendy
characters.description: The flaky"town bicycle"nurse of the school, and Moralton's hospital. Absurdly Youthful Mother:She's the biological mother of Joe Secondopinionson. What makes her youthful is the fact she gave birth to Joe when she wastwelveyears old. Because You Were Nice to Me: Granted, Joe may be the neighborhood bully, but he is the only one who respects Nurse Bendy in contrast to the other residents seeing her as a sex toy. This gets her and Joe to bond.He then beats the crap out of his father for lying about her, making it clear that he loves his mom. Brainless Beauty:Deconstructedwhen she shows that she really wants to have children and raise a normal family, but the stereotype of being a nymphomaniac keeps her from fulfilling it.Trauma from sexual assault as a child has also lead to her clinging to childish turns of phrases and pass times, which lends itself to this initial viewing of her. Dark and Troubled Past: As a child, she wasa statutory rape victim to someone60 years her senior, and was evenimpregnatedby him. After giving birth to Joe, she was forced to give up her kid to that rapist because he had the means to raise the kid but didn't take her in (which, to be honest, is for the best if he continued to assault her) and the only way she kept tabs on Joe was from his older sister.

characters.name: Dr. Potterswheel
characters.description: The town's gynecologist, who's obsessed with gore. Comically Inept Healing: He's a pretty terrible doctor when it comes down to it. When Orel has a gunshot wound to the foot, his advice is to eat a lot of fatty foods to clog up his arteries, thereby minimizing bleeding. This is played for laughs at first, but not so much when it starts to sink in how having such an inept doctor hurts the town.Dr. Potterswheel:(Looking at a flat-lining Orel)It isn't healthy to be dead that long. Dr. Feelgood: Which might have resulted in his wife's death—he kept giving her painkillers rather than actually treating her wounds. Dr. Jerk: Very snarky and not really good at his job, all things considered. Hidden Depths:In "Innocence", he's the one who first points out that Orel may have been misled by the collective bad advice of all the Moralton citizens.When Clay tries provoking him into a fight, most notably insulting his dead wife and his malpractice. Potterswheel and the others just blow him off rather than stoop to his level.When Clay rambles about his dead wife, he seems regretful and aware about how badly he screwed up with her.Similarly he stops Papermouth from pistol-whipping Clay after Clay hits hisBerserk Button.

characters.name: Ms. Secondopinionson
characters.description: Joe's older half-sister who has a very masculine voice. Ambiguous Gender Identity: She masks her deep baritone voice when speaking to anybody outside of her father or brother with a more feminine affect (separating her from some other women in the show whose manly voices are just gags) and gives the reason as to why she can't have children as simply "I'm barren". She might very well be a trans woman, which, if anybody outside of her family knew, would almost definitely be a social (or maybe literal) death sentence in Moralton. Beware the Nice Ones: Downplayed. She is extremely patient and tolerant of Joe and willing to care for her Alzheimer's father, but pointedly, does absolutely nothing to stop Joe from beating the shit out of their father once Joe's discovered the truth about his mom,with the obvious implication she let it happen because she knew that Dr. Secondopinionson raped Nurse Bendy when she was 12 and likely knowing it's useless to stop her brother anyway. Cool Big Sis: She tries to be this for her brother Joe, although Joe doesn't care and argues with her, yelling at her she's not his mom. She's also willing to make his favorite dessert too, although he was too angry to taste it when he came backbecause he went to beat up his dad for lying about his mom. She gently told him that the reason their dad never told Joe about Nurse Bendy was that it was the first memory he forgot after getting Alzheimer's. She was also willing to allow Joe to go back to his mom, seeing that Joe and Nurse Bendy would be happy together. Deadpan Snarker: She's prone to some pretty dry quips. Considering how much of aBratty Half-PintJoe is, it's not hard to see why.Joe:Don't tell me what to do! You're not my mom! Unh!Ms. Secondopinionson:I know that. I'm no one's mom.Joe:'Cause you're ugly!Ms. Secondopinionson:No. Because I'm barren.Joe:Then why aren't you married?Ms. Secondopinionson:That could be because I'm ugly.

characters.name: Principal Fakey
characters.description: The School's principal, who is having an affair with Nurse Bendy. Harmful to Minors:After finding out he caught anSTD, he walks around school with his pants down while angrily screaming.Is there not decency left in this world!?. Hate Sink: Not as bad as Clay or Cecil, but the show makes it clear that he's not meant to be liked. He's a hypocritical, misogynistic adulterer who regularly cheats on his wife with Nurse Bendy. Whilst there were instances of him feeling guilty of his actions, it was mostly out of fear of him getting caught or being forced to be held accountable for what he did, evidenced by how he continued going through with his affair. Implausible Deniability: Does this to himself after taking Orel's advice, saying that he's been as faithful as everas he's having sex with Nurse Bendy. Insane Troll Logic: Upon finding out he caught an STD, he ends up believing it's becausehis wifehas been cheating. The fact that he caught it from Nurse Bendy (who he's been having an affair with, and who has multiple partners) never even crosses his mind.

characters.name: Sal Figurelli
characters.description: The owner of his own shop and the father of Billy Figurelli. Ambiguous Situation: It’s unknown why Sal’s wife slaps him and leaves him in "Maturity", nor is it said how or why they got back together in "God’s Image". Awful Wedded Life: Downplayed. While they don’t have aSexless Marriage, Mr. and Mrs. Figurelli are always shown having arguments with one another and Sal once bought prostitutes to have sex with despite having a wife. That being said, they do care for each other if Sal’s protection of his family in "God’s Image" is anything to go by. Catchphrase: “No prob, Bob!” Or sometimes just “No prob.” Domestic Abuse: On the receiving end from his wife, forunknown reasons, though it’s impliedthis is an isolated circumstance.In the original cut of the episode, Sal actually fights back by punching her in the face after getting slapped; this scene was removed from most releases but kept in the Russian dub.

characters.name: Officer Papermouth
characters.description: Chubby Chaser: Roger was married to Florence Papermouth,a rather large and overweight woman, and given that it’s implied at times that he wants her back, it’s unlikely that he ever had problems with her weight. Disproportionate Retribution: Towards Florence and Doughy.When Officer Papermouth sees his recent ex-wife, Florence, taking Rev. Putty up to her apartment to be intimate, he takes his daughter's beloved Teddy bear (a gift from Florence) while she's sleeping and empties his gun into it. For what it's worth,he immediately regrets it afterwards.He tried to put a bullet in Doughy after he drove away his date on accident. When Officer Papermouth sees his recent ex-wife, Florence, taking Rev. Putty up to her apartment to be intimate, he takes his daughter's beloved Teddy bear (a gift from Florence) while she's sleeping and empties his gun into it. For what it's worth,he immediately regrets it afterwards. He tried to put a bullet in Doughy after he drove away his date on accident. Everyone Has Standards: When Clay goes off on him over Papermouth's own insecurities, he just takes it. When Clay makes a derogatory comment to the bartender, Dolly, hereto shown only as a friendly individual to all of them, Papermouth slams Clay's head on the bar and is about to pistol whip him when Dr. Potterswheel stops him.

characters.name: Florence Papermouth
characters.description: Officer Papermouth's ex-wife. Abhorrent Admirer: To Reverend Putty.Until "Sundays" completely dashes away her expectations of him. Ascended Extra: She's merely a background character often seen at Church and occasionally in Moralton throughout the whole show. It isn't until "Sundays" that we actually learn about her (which features most of her appearances). Big Eater: "Sundays" shows she binge eats, likely explaining why she’s overweight. A Day in the Limelight: "Sundays."

characters.name: Dottie
characters.description: Florence's friend and roommate. Abusive Parents: "Love" shows her angrily spanking her daughter in public after the little girl saysshe misses her dad. A Day in the Limelight: "Sundays", where we get more characterization out of her and Florence. Everyone Has Standards: When Dottie listens in on Florence and Rev. Putty having sex, she initially smiles when Putty cries out her name instead. However, even she's visibly taken aback when Putty immediately makes it clear that he regrets doing the deed with Florence, causing the latter to cry. Fat and Skinny: Skinny to Florence's Fat. At one point, Dottie mocks the latter because of her weight while claiming it to be constructive criticism.

characters.name: Kim and Karl
characters.description: Abusive Parents: To Doughy; they make it open that they don't like him and want him out of the house, never show him affection, sometimes don't even feed him, andtrick him into sitting outside his house for a whole dayby hiding the key. Corrupted Character Copy: They’re fractured copies of Archie and Betty. While they are identical to Archie and Betty in terms of appearance and relationship, Kim and Karl show what would happen if the two were selfish, obnoxious, and manipulative towards others. Kim and Karl also deconstruct how awful it would be if Archie and Betty realistically stuck to theirSickening Sweetheartsroutine into adulthood, as they neglect their responsibilities as both adults and parents. Early-Installment Weirdness: In the first episode, Doughy claims "cleanliness is next to godliness" is something his mother says, making it sound like his parents are akin to Oral's in strictness. While not totally out of the realm of possibility, this certainly doesn't sound like the Kim we would eventually meet. Expy: Only the character designs that are identical to Archie and Betty fromArchie Comics. Girl-on-Girl is Hot: Karl was attracted to Kim pretending to have feelings for Stephanie and kissing her.
